title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) with this easy-to-follow recipe. Hearty, healthy, and packed with flavor. Perfect for any meal!
longTailKeywords: how to make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas
easy Lebanese Red Lentil Soup recipe
authentic Middle Eastern Red Lentil Soup
lowCompetitionKeywords: Middle Eastern red lentil soup with lemon
seo.h1: Authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?
Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Adas Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a comforting bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas), a staple of Middle Eastern cuisine.

description: This comforting Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is a beloved staple in Middle Eastern cuisine. It's a nourishing and flavorful dish, perfect for a hearty meal, especially on cooler days. Made with simple ingredients, this vegan-friendly soup offers a delightful blend of earthy lentils, aromatic spices, and a bright lemon finish.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Aromatics
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear; drain well. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Sauté Vegetables and Spices
instructions.text: Add the minced garlic, diced carrots, and diced potatoes to the pot. Sauté for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables start to soften slightly. Stir in the ground cumin and ground turmeric, cooking for 1 minute more until fragrant.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot. Pour in the vegetable broth (or water), salt, and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender.

instructions.title: Achieve Desired Consistency
instructions.text: Once cooked, you can leave the soup as is for a rustic texture, or use an immersion blender to partially or fully purée the soup to your desired consistency. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth to thin it out.

instructions.title: Finish with Lemon
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the freshly squeezed lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, adding more salt, pepper, or lemon juice to your preference. The lemon is crucial for that authentic Lebanese flavor.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve immediately with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil and additional lemon wedges on the side for an extra zesty kick.

tips: For extra depth of flavor, toast the cumin and turmeric in a dry pan for a minute before adding to the soup. This brings out their aromatic qualities even more.

If you prefer a smoother soup but dont have an immersion blender, carefully transfer batches of the soup to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot. Be cautious with hot liquids.
Adas soup thickens as it cools. If you have leftovers, you might need to add a splash of water or broth when reheating to achieve the desired consistency.
article:
Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known affectionately as Adas, is a culinary embrace in a bowl. This humble yet incredibly flavorful dish stands as a testament to the nourishing power of simple ingredients, deeply rooted in Middle Eastern culinary traditions. For generations, Adas has graced tables across Lebanon and beyond, cherished for its comforting warmth, robust flavor, and remarkable nutritional profile. Whether it's a chilly winter evening, a hearty lunch, or a light Iftar meal during Ramadan, this vibrant, lemony soup offers solace and satisfaction.

This article delves into the heart of Adas, guiding you through its origins, essential ingredients, and the step-by-step process of crafting your own bowl of this liquid gold. Prepare to discover why Lebanese Red Lentil Soup isn't just a meal, but a cherished experience.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?

Adas, meaning "lentils" in Arabic, refers to a traditional Lebanese Red Lentil Soup that is a cornerstone of Middle Eastern cuisine. Unlike some richer, meat-based stews, Adas is celebrated for its vegetarian nature and its ability to deliver profound flavor from minimal components. It's typically a smooth, creamy soup, characterized by its beautiful orange-red hue, derived from the split red lentils.

The heart and soul of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup lies in its simplicity and the masterful balance of earthy lentils with bright, zesty lemon. Often enhanced with aromatic spices like cumin and a fresh flourish of mint, it’s a soup that feels both incredibly wholesome and surprisingly sophisticated. Beyond its delicious taste, Adas is a nutritional powerhouse, packed with plant-based protein, dietary fiber, iron, and various B vitamins, making it an excellent choice for a healthy diet. It’s a dish that embodies frugality and flavor, proving that some of the best meals come from the most basic ingredients.

## Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

To truly capture the authentic taste of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, quality ingredients are key, though the list itself is wonderfully short and accessible. Here’s what you’ll need to gather:

*   **Red Split Lentils:** The star of the show. These cook quickly and break down easily, creating the soup's signature creamy texture. Ensure they are well-rinsed.
*   **Olive Oil:** A foundational element in Lebanese cooking, used for sautéing and as a finishing drizzle.
*   **Onion:** Finely chopped, provides a sweet and aromatic base.
*   **Garlic:** Minced, adds pungent depth and characteristic Middle Eastern flavor.
*   **Carrots:** (Optional, but highly recommended) Diced carrots add natural sweetness, an extra layer of flavor, and boost the soup's vibrant color and nutritional value.
*   **Vegetable Broth or Water:** The liquid base. Broth adds more depth, but water works perfectly well, allowing the lentil and lemon flavors to shine.
*   **Spices:**
    *   **Cumin:** Essential for that earthy, warm, and distinctly Middle Eastern flavor.
    *   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To season and enhance all the other flavors.
    *   **Turmeric:** (Optional) A pinch can intensify the soup's golden-red color and add a subtle warmth.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice:** Non-negotiable! Added at the end, it provides the crucial bright, zesty tang that defines Adas.
*   **Fresh Mint:** For garnish. Its refreshing aroma and taste perfectly complement the richness of the lentils and the sharpness of the lemon.

## How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step

Mastering the art of making Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is surprisingly straightforward. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl every time:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Start by thoroughly rinsing your red split lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy. Drain well.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a generous drizzle of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until it becomes soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and diced carrots (if using), and cook for another 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
3.  **Add Lentils and Spices:** Stir in the rinsed lentils, cumin, salt, pepper, and turmeric (if using). Sauté for another minute, allowing the lentils to toast slightly and absorb the flavors of the aromatics.
4.  **Add Liquid and Simmer:** Pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer gently for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Blend to Smoothness:** Once the lentils are cooked, remove the pot from the heat. Use an immersion blender directly in the pot to blend the soup until it reaches your desired smooth and creamy consistency. Alternatively, carefully transfer batches of the soup to a regular blender (be cautious with hot liquids) and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.
6.  **Finish and Season:** Return the pot to low heat. Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Taste the soup and adjust the seasoning as needed, adding more salt, pepper, or lemon juice to your preference. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth to reach your desired consistency.
7.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Garnish each serving with a sprinkle of fresh chopped mint and a final drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

## Tips for the Best Adas Soup

While the recipe for Adas is simple, a few expert tips can elevate your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup from good to truly exceptional:

*   **Rinse Those Lentils!** Don't skip this step. Rinsing removes starch and any potential impurities, ensuring a cleaner flavor and texture.
*   **Fresh Lemon is Non-Negotiable:** Bottled lemon juice simply won't do. The bright, fresh acidity of freshly squeezed lemon is paramount to balancing the earthiness of the lentils. Add it at the end to preserve its vibrant flavor.
*   **Don't Skimp on Olive Oil:** Use good quality extra virgin olive oil, both for sautéing and as a finishing drizzle. It adds a luxurious mouthfeel and rich flavor.
*   **Cumin is Key:** Cumin is the signature spice for Adas. Ensure your cumin is fresh for the most aromatic results.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** This is crucial. Lentils can absorb a lot of salt, and the amount of lemon needed can vary. Taste before serving and adjust salt, pepper, and lemon juice until it sings.
*   **Achieve the Right Consistency:** Adas should be creamy but not overly thick. If it's too dense after blending, thin it out with a little hot water or vegetable broth until it flows smoothly off your spoon.
*   **Consider a "Ta'liyeh" (Garlic-Mint Sizzle):** For an extra burst of flavor, some cooks prepare a "ta'liyeh" – a small amount of minced garlic and fresh mint sautéed in olive oil until fragrant, then stirred into the finished soup or drizzled over individual servings.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is wonderfully versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways, whether as a comforting main course or a flavorful appetizer.

*   **Classic Garnishes:** Always serve Adas hot, garnished with a generous sprinkle of fresh chopped mint and a final swirl of good quality extra virgin olive oil. A wedge of lemon on the side allows diners to add more zest to their liking.
*   **Bread is Essential:** Warm pita bread is the perfect accompaniment for dipping and scooping up every last drop of the delicious soup. Toasted pita bread or pita chips also add a lovely crunch.
*   **Simple Sides:** A fresh green salad, like a simple cucumber and tomato salad with a lemon-mint dressing, provides a refreshing contrast. Pickled vegetables (torshi) are also a traditional pairing, offering a tangy counterpoint.
*   **Spice it Up:** For those who enjoy a little heat, a pinch of red pepper flakes or a dash of hot sauce can be added to individual bowls.
*   **A Full Meal:** While light, Adas is incredibly filling due to the lentils. It can easily stand alone as a light lunch or dinner, perhaps served with some labneh (strained yogurt) on the side.

## Variations & Dietary Adaptations

While the classic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, it's also wonderfully adaptable to different tastes and dietary needs.

*   **Vegan and Gluten-Free:** Adas is naturally vegan (when using vegetable broth or water) and gluten-free, making it an excellent choice for a wide range of diets.
*   **Spicier Kick:** To add a touch of heat, incorporate a pinch of cayenne pepper or a finely minced fresh green chili along with the other spices.
*   **Heartier Options:** For a more substantial soup, you can add finely diced potatoes or zucchini along with the carrots. They will cook down and add body to the soup.
*   **Different Herbs:** While fresh mint is traditional and highly recommended, you can experiment with other fresh herbs like cilantro or parsley for a slightly different flavor profile. Add them towards the end of cooking or as a garnish.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A tiny pinch of smoked paprika can add a subtle smoky depth, though it's not traditional.
*   **Creamier Texture (Non-Traditional):** For an even richer, creamier texture, a small amount of dairy-free cream or coconut milk could be stirred in at the very end, though this deviates from the authentic preparation.

Embrace the warmth and nutrition of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, a dish that truly embodies the heart of Middle Eastern hospitality. Its simplicity belies a depth of flavor that is both comforting and invigorating. This recipe for Adas isn't just about cooking; it's about connecting with a rich culinary heritage and bringing wholesome, delicious food to your table. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to savor one of the most beloved soups from the Levant.
